name: PVS-Studio Static Code Analyzer
what_it_does: Static code analyzer for C, C++, C#, and Java — enabling development teams to detect bugs (undefined behavior, memory errors, concurrency issues) and security vulnerabilities, with IDE integration (Visual Studio, Rider, IntelliJ, CLion), CI/CD pipeline integration, MISRA/OWASP/CWE compliance checking, and a unique free license model for open-source projects.
use_cases: Detecting C++ undefined behavior and memory errors via PVS-Studio in CI/CD pipelines
Checking C# and Java code for security vulnerabilities via PVS-Studio for .NET/JVM teams
Enforcing MISRA C/C++ compliance via PVS-Studio for embedded systems development
Integrating PVS-Studio reports into CI/CD via SARIF/JSON output for pipeline quality gates
Auditing open-source C/C++ projects via PVS-Studio free open-source license
not_for: Enterprise SAST with centralized server management (use Klocwork or Coverity for enterprise)
Languages beyond C, C++, C#, Java (use Semgrep for multi-language coverage)
SCA/dependency scanning (PVS-Studio is source code analysis, not OSS vulnerability scanning)
best_when: A C++, C#, or Java team needs a high-quality static analyzer with MISRA support and free open-source licensing — PVS-Studio has a strong reputation for finding real bugs vs false positives.
avoid_when: You need centralized enterprise SAST management, SCA, or analysis of languages beyond C/C++/C#/Java.

auth.notes: License file (pvs-studio.cfg) for commercial use. Free license for open-source projects (GitHub-verified). No API service — CLI tool (pvs-studio-analyzer for Linux, pvs-studio.exe for Windows). Visual Studio, CLion, JetBrains Rider plugins. No network API calls for analysis. Output: plog file → convert to SARIF/JSON/HTML via plogConverter. No REST API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for open-source projects (license from pvs-studio.com/en/order/)
pricing.paid_tiers: Commercial license: $4,990/year per developer (team packs available)
pricing.notes: Russian company (Tula, Russia). Founded 2008. Geopolitical risk: Russian company under sanctions context — verify vendor compliance for your jurisdiction. Originally VivaMP, rebranded PVS-Studio. C/C++ analysis pioneer in Russia/CIS. Free open-source license: write special comment (// This is an open source non-commercial project) or apply via website. MISRA C 2004/2012, OWASP Top 10 for C/C++, CWE. Incremental analysis. Can analyze Linux kernel, FreeBSD, Chromium (published bug reports as marketing). SARIF output for GitHub integration. V-number diagnostic codes (V501, V595, etc.). False positive suppression via inline comments. IDE support: VS, VS Code, CLion, Eclipse, Rider.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI tool only — no API service. License file auth. Russian company (geopolitical risk). Free for open-source. MISRA/CWE/OWASP. SARIF output. V-number diagnostic codes. IDE plugins.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Analysis is deterministic for same source. Output is .plog file. Fully idempotent for same input.
known_agent_gotchas: Russian company: geopolitical risk — verify sanctions compliance for your organization's jurisdiction
No REST API — local CLI tool: pvs-studio-analyzer analyze (Linux) or pvs-studio.exe (Windows)
Two-step: analyze → plogConverter to convert .plog to SARIF/JSON/HTML for CI
Free open-source license: apply at pvs-studio.com/en/order/ (GitHub repo required)
Incremental analysis: pvs-studio-analyzer analyze --incremental-build (faster on CI)
V-number codes (V501, V595, etc.) — use as suppression identifiers in code comments

security_score_components.security_notes: No credentials for analysis — license file only. Russian company: geopolitical risk (sanctions, data sovereignty). Code analyzed locally — no source code transmitted. MISRA/OWASP/CWE compliance checking. License validation may phone home for commercial licenses — verify network behavior. Sanctions compliance risk: verify before deploying in regulated environments.
